(ii) The Lagna at Coitus is as much removed from the Moon
at birth as the Moon is from Birth lagna.
(iii) 'The lagna at Coitus may be the midpoint of the lagna
and the Moon at birth".
(iv) 'The lagna at coitus may be the midpoint of the Sun and
the Moon", when the birth is irregular or premature.
One or two ofthese principles may apply. But extensive
verification is needed.
We now give the method followed by the advocates of the
Pre-Natal Epoch.
6. Three factors are necessary for conception. These are
ovulation, impregnation, and the fixation of the impregnated
ovum. These are governed by the Moon. The position of the
Moon in this period has a close relation to the sensitive points
in the woman's chart and also with the Ascendant or Descen
dant (seventh house) of the child's birth chart.
Fertilization takes nine days. If fecundation does not take
place within fourteen days after menstruation, it ceases to
function.
